Title: Pan-genome analysis identifies intersecting roles for Pseudomonas specialized metabolites in potato pathogen inhibition

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Assembles the peptide-lipid scaffold of a viscosin-like cyclic lipopeptide (viscosin I) by NRPS-mediated incorporation of amino acid residues and cyclization
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: 3-hydroxydecanoic acid-linked peptide intermediates; amino acids including Leu, Glu, Thr, Val, Ser, Ile
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Suppresses potato pathogen growth; contributes to swarming motility and microbial competitive colonization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes viscosin I production, swarming motility, and inhibition of Streptomyces scabies, Phytophthora infestans, and Gaeumannomyces graminis in plate assays
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key pathway gene required for product formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In-frame deletion mutant; LC-MS/MS metabolite loss; swarming assay; cross-streak assay; lux-tagged colonization imaging; purified metabolite disk diffusion assay; NMR/MS structural characterization
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: On-plate antagonism assays; glasshouse potato scab assay
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome sequencing, ENA PRJEB34261; metabolomics/mass spectrometry, MassIVE MSV000084283
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PS682_04206 is required for biosynthesis of the Ps682 viscosin-like CLP named viscosin I. 2. Deletion abolished the m/z 1126.69 metabolite and swarming motility. 3. The mutant lost on-plate inhibition of S. scabies and lost activity against P. infestans and G. graminis, supporting viscosin I as the causal suppressive metabolite.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The product was named viscosin I because it is a viscosin isomer and absolute configuration could not be conclusively assigned; in planta contribution could not be determined because Ps682 itself was not significantly protective in potato assays.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering or selecting broad-spectrum biocontrol pseudomonads that produce CLPs with direct pathogen inhibition and motility advantages.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: other viscosin-like BGC NRPS and transporter/regulatory proteins

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Assembles a tensin-like cyclic lipopeptide by NRPS-mediated peptide-lipid assembly
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Amino acid building blocks predicted from NRPS specificity modules plus lipid starter unit
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Suppresses potato scab pathogen growth/development and promotes Pseudomonas motility/competitive colonization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes motility and S. scabies inhibition on plates and is required for in planta potato scab biocontrol by Ps619
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key pathway gene required for product formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In-frame deletion mutant; LC-MS metabolite loss; cross-streak assay; cryo-SEM; potato scab biocontrol assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: On-plate antagonism assays under standard and drier conditions; glasshouse infection assay on potato cv. Maris Piper
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome sequencing, ENA PRJEB34261; metabolomics/mass spectrometry, MassIVE MSV000084283
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PS619_02963 is required for production of a tensin-like CLP in Ps619. 2. Loss of this gene reduces motility and suppressive activity toward S. scabies. 3. Unlike HCN, tensin is essential for Ps619-mediated suppression of potato scab in planta.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A tensin BGC had not previously been reported; the metabolite also induced a developmental defect in S. scabies independent of motility.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for constructing or selecting potato biocontrol inoculants, especially in combination with field deployment strategies.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: other tensin-like BGC proteins; hcnABC shows additive phenotypic interaction

Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Ps619: PS619_05844 (hcnB); PS619_05845 (hcnC); hcnA not individually listed
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Pseudomonas spp. field isolates; validated in Pseudomonas sp. Ps619
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts glycine into hydrogen cyanide and carbon dioxide/ammonia via the membrane-associated HCN synthase complex
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Long-range inhibition of plant pathogens including S. scabies
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Volatile extracellular environment surrounding bacterial colonies
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes volatile, long-range inhibition of S. scabies on plates and acts additively with CLPs, but is not required for Ps619 potato scab suppression in planta
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key pathway gene cluster required for product formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Correlation analysis; Feigl-Anger HCN detection assay; in-frame deletion of hcnBC in Ps619; cross-streak assays; barrier assays; cryo-SEM; potato biocontrol assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: On-plate antagonism assays; drier plate conditions; barrier-separated co-culture; glasshouse potato assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Presence of hcnABC strongly correlates with pathogen suppression and nearly all strains carrying the cluster produce HCN. 2. Deletion in Ps619 reduces long-range inhibition of S. scabies and contributes additively with tensin to total suppression. 3. HCN is dispensable for Ps619-mediated potato scab biocontrol in planta.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: HCN acts at a distance because it is volatile; the paper notes pseudomonads tolerate cyanide through cyanide-insensitive respiration.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential broad toxicity to non-target organisms is implied but not directly tested.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for broad-spectrum antagonism in vitro, but not sufficient as a standalone predictor of in planta potato scab control.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: HcnA; HcnB; HcnC; phenotypic additivity with tensin pathway
